mainEntity.name: Who needs tax audit under Section 44AB?

acceptedAnswer.text: Businesses with turnover exceeding Rs 1 crore (Rs 10 crores if cash receipts and payments are within 5% of total). Professionals with gross receipts exceeding Rs 50 lakhs. Also applicable when presumptive taxation under Section 44AD/44ADA is opted but income is below the prescribed percentage.

mainEntity.name: What is the due date for tax audit report?

acceptedAnswer.text: 30 September of the assessment year (e.g., 30 September 2026 for FY 2025-26). If transfer pricing provisions apply, the due date is 31 October. Late filing of tax audit report attracts penalty of 0.5% of turnover or Rs 1.5 lakhs, whichever is lower, under Section 271B.

mainEntity.name: Can the same CA do both statutory audit and tax audit?

acceptedAnswer.text: For companies, the statutory auditor typically also does the tax audit using Form 3CA. For non-corporate entities, any practising CA can be appointed as tax auditor. There is no restriction on the same CA performing both audits.

faqs.q: Who needs tax audit under Section 44AB?
faqs.a: Businesses with turnover exceeding Rs 1 crore (Rs 10 crores if cash receipts and payments are within 5% of total). Professionals with gross receipts exceeding Rs 50 lakhs. Also applicable when presumptive taxation under Section 44AD/44ADA is opted but income is below the prescribed percentage.

faqs.q: What is the due date for tax audit report?
faqs.a: 30 September of the assessment year (e.g., 30 September 2026 for FY 2025-26). If transfer pricing provisions apply, the due date is 31 October. Late filing of tax audit report attracts penalty of 0.5% of turnover or Rs 1.5 lakhs, whichever is lower, under Section 271B.

faqs.q: Can the same CA do both statutory audit and tax audit?
faqs.a: For companies, the statutory auditor typically also does the tax audit using Form 3CA. For non-corporate entities, any practising CA can be appointed as tax auditor. There is no restriction on the same CA performing both audits.
